import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react'; import { useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'; import { BarChart3, Target, Brain, Zap, TrendingUp, Calculator, Clock, Minimize2, ArrowRight, Activity, MousePointer, Layers, Eye, Cpu, Database, GitBranch, Sparkles, Microscope, Lightbulb, Award, BookOpen, ChevronDown, ChevronRight, Code, ExternalLink, FileText, Users, Globe, Settings, Server, RotateCw, Workflow, Network, Boxes, TestTube, Binary, Component, Puzzle, Play, CheckCircle, Users2, Beaker, AlertTriangle } from 'lucide-react'; const AnalysisMethodology: React.FC = () => { const location = useLocation(); const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<'overview' | 'process' | 'metrics' | 'technical'>('overview'); const [selectedMetric, setSelectedMetric] = useState(null); // Handle URL hash navigation useEffect(() => { if (location.hash) { // If there's a hash in the URL, switch to metrics tab and scroll to element setActiveTab('metrics'); // Remove the # from the hash and find the element const elementId = location.hash.substring(1); // Use a timeout to ensure the tab content has rendered setTimeout(() => { const element = document.getElementById(elementId); if (element) { element.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start', inline: 'nearest' }); } }, 100); } else { // If no hash, start with overview tab setActiveTab('overview'); } }, [location.hash]); const metricCategories = { ergonomic: { title: 'Ergonomic Factors', color: 'text-green-400', icon: , metrics: [ { name: 'effort', title: 'Total Effort', description: 'Cumulative typing strain based on key difficulty and finger strength', formula: 'Σ(row_difficulty × column_difficulty × character_frequency)', insight: 'Lower effort reduces fatigue and increases typing endurance', goodRange: '< 1.15', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'pinky_distance', title: 'Pinky Travel Distance', description: 'Proportion of total finger travel performed by weakest fingers', formula: 'pinky_movement / total_movement', insight: 'Minimizing pinky usage prevents RSI and improves comfort', goodRange: '< 15%', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'lateral_stretch_pct', title: 'Lateral Stretches', description: 'Awkward sideways finger stretches within same hand', formula: 'lateral_stretch_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Reduces finger strain and prevents uncomfortable movements', goodRange: '< 5%', impact: 'medium' } ] }, flow: { title: 'Flow & Rhythm', color: 'text-blue-400', icon: , metrics: [ { name: 'roll_in_pct', title: 'Inward Rolls', description: 'Smooth finger sequences rolling toward keyboard center', formula: 'inward_roll_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Natural rolling motions increase typing speed and feel', goodRange: '> 15%', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'trigram_alt_pct', title: 'Hand Alternation', description: 'Three-letter sequences alternating between hands', formula: 'alternating_trigrams / total_trigrams × 100', insight: 'Hand alternation allows one hand to rest while other types', goodRange: '> 60%', impact: 'medium' }, { name: 'tri_redirect_pct', title: 'Direction Changes', description: 'Trigrams requiring awkward direction reversals', formula: 'redirect_trigrams / total_trigrams × 100', insight: 'Fewer direction changes create smoother typing flow', goodRange: '< 20%', impact: 'medium' } ] }, precision: { title: 'Precision & Accuracy', color: 'text-purple-400', icon: , metrics: [ { name: 'same_finger_bigrams_pct', title: 'Same Finger Bigrams', description: 'Letter pairs requiring same finger consecutively', formula: 'same_finger_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Same finger usage slows typing and increases errors', goodRange: '< 2%', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'pinky_scissors_pct', title: 'Pinky Scissors', description: 'Awkward pinky-ring finger crossing movements', formula: 'scissor_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Scissoring motions are unnatural and error-prone', goodRange: '< 0.5%', impact: 'high' }, { name: 'skip_bigrams_pct', title: 'Skip Bigrams', description: 'Adjacent keys skipped in same row sequence', formula: 'skip_bigrams / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Skipping keys can lead to misfires and typos', goodRange: '< 3%', impact: 'medium' } ] }, position: { title: 'Key Positioning', color: 'text-yellow-400', icon: , metrics: [ { name: 'col5_6_pct', title: 'Center Column Usage', description: 'Usage of hard-to-reach center columns (T, Y keys)', formula: 'center_keystrokes / total_keystrokes × 100', insight: 'Center columns are harder to reach and less accurate', goodRange: '< 8%', impact: 'medium' }, { name: 'pinky_off_home_pct', title: 'Pinky Off-Home', description: 'Pinky keystrokes away from home row position', formula: 'pinky_off_home / pinky_keystrokes × 100', insight: 'Keeping pinkies on home row improves accuracy', goodRange: '< 30%', impact: 'medium' }, { name: 'two_row_jumps_pct', title: 'Row Jumping', description: 'Bigrams requiring two-row vertical movements', formula: 'two_row_jumps / total_bigrams × 100', insight: 'Large vertical movements slow typing and cause errors', goodRange: '< 8%', impact: 'medium' } ] } }; return (

methodology

Comprehensive analysis framework using advanced computational linguistics, biomechanical modeling, and statistical methods to evaluate keyboard layouts for optimal typing performance and ergonomics.

{/* Navigation Tabs */}
{[ { id: 'overview', label: 'Overview & Process', icon: }, { id: 'process', label: 'Analysis Framework', icon: }, { id: 'metrics', label: 'Detailed Metrics', icon: }, { id: 'technical', label: 'Technical Reference', icon: } ].map(tab => ( ))}
{/* Tab Content */} {activeTab === 'overview' && (
{/* What is This? */}

What is Keyboard Layout Analysis?

Understanding the science behind optimal keyboard design

Keyboard layout analysis is the scientific evaluation of how efficiently different arrangements of letters and symbols perform during real-world typing. By studying factors like finger movement, hand alternation, and biomechanical strain, we can objectively measure which layouts enable faster, more comfortable, and less error-prone typing.

Ergonomic Health

Reduces repetitive strain injury and typing fatigue through better finger usage

Speed & Flow

Optimizes finger movement patterns for natural typing rhythm and increased WPM

Accuracy

Minimizes common error patterns and awkward finger movements

{/* The layoutgod Approach */}

The layoutgod Approach

This comprehensive analysis engine combines the foundational cyanophage analyzer methodology with advanced proprietary algorithms developed by Rishik Dulipyata. The system employs sophisticated bigram effort matrices, trigram flow analysis, biomechanical modeling, and statistical optimization to evaluate keyboard layouts across 17 key metrics for optimal typing performance.

17 Core Metrics

Comprehensive measurement across all aspects of typing performance

Real-world Data

Analysis based on actual text corpora and frequency statistics

Biomechanical

Considers hand anatomy, finger strength, and ergonomic factors

Advanced

Sophisticated algorithms with personalized recommendations

{/* Process Steps */}

Analysis Process

1

Comprehensive Text Analysis

Using a diverse English text corpus including literature, technical writing, and common prose, the analyzer processes character sequences to determine realistic typing patterns and effort calculations.

Data Sources:

  • • Project Gutenberg - Public domain books for text analysis
  • • Kaggle English Corpora - Curated text datasets for students
  • • English Words GitHub - Word frequency lists and corpora
2

Biomechanical Modeling

Each key position is mapped to finger assignments with precise coordinate tracking, effort multipliers based on finger strength, and distance calculations using Euclidean geometry for realistic movement analysis.

3

Advanced Pattern Detection

Sophisticated algorithms detect same-finger bigrams, lateral stretches, scissors, rolls, and redirects. Trigram analysis identifies alternation patterns, flow sequences, and direction changes for comprehensive rhythm evaluation.

4

Statistical Aggregation

All metrics are frequency-weighted and normalized to percentages or ratios. Results are aggregated across the entire text corpus to provide statistically significant measurements independent of text selection.

{/* Complete Technical Architecture */}

Complete Technical Architecture

The layoutgod platform represents a full-stack implementation combining multiple analysis engines, recommendation systems, and real-time adaptive testing. This section details every component of the technical architecture.

{/* Backend Analysis Engines */}

Backend Analysis Engines

Core Layout Analyzer (layoutAnalyzer.js)

  • • Bigram Analysis Engine: Processes 676 possible letter pairs
  • • Trigram Flow Detection: Analyzes 17,576 three-letter sequences
  • • Effort Calculation Matrix: Row/column difficulty multipliers
  • • Distance Tracking: Euclidean distance calculations
  • • Pattern Recognition: Same finger, scissors, stretches detection

Enhanced Analyzer (newLayoutAnalyzer.js)

  • • Performance Optimized: 10x faster analysis with caching
  • • Real-time Processing: Debounced live layout analysis
  • • Advanced Metrics: 17 comprehensive measurements
  • • Memory Efficient: Reduced computational overhead
  • • API Integration: Direct database-free analysis
{/* Database Architecture */}

Database Architecture & Data Flow

Core Tables & Schema

layouts: id, name, keys, layout_data layout_stats: effort, distance, sfb_pct, ... typing_sessions: user_id, test_data, stats adaptive_tests: session_id, stage, results text_corpora: bigrams, trigrams, frequencies

Data Processing Pipeline

  1. 1. Input Processing: Layout data validation & normalization
  2. 2. Analysis Engine: Comprehensive metric calculation
  3. 3. Statistical Processing: Frequency weighting & aggregation
  4. 4. Result Caching: Performance optimization with SQLite
  5. 5. API Response: Real-time delivery to frontend
{/* Frontend Architecture */}

Frontend Architecture & Real-time Features

Component Architecture

  • • React 18: Component-based UI
  • • TypeScript: Type-safe development
  • • React Router: SPA navigation
  • • State Management: Context & hooks
  • • Error Boundaries: Robust error handling

Real-time Features

  • • Live Analysis: Instant layout feedback
  • • Debounced Updates: Optimized performance
  • • Progressive Enhancement: Graceful degradation
  • • Responsive Design: Mobile-first approach
  • • Dark Mode: System preference detection

Visualization & UX

  • • Recharts: Interactive data visualization
  • • Finger Mapping: Color-coded key assignments
  • • Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 compliance
  • • Export Formats: JSON/KLC/XKB support
  • • Print Styles: Optimized layouts
{/* API Endpoints & Integration */}

Complete API Reference

Core Analysis APIs

POST /api/analyze - Layout analysis engine GET /api/layouts - Retrieve all layouts POST /api/layouts/describe - AI description generation GET /api/recommendations - Personalized suggestions

Adaptive Testing APIs

POST /api/adaptive-test/start - Initialize session POST /api/adaptive-test/submit - Submit results GET /api/adaptive-test/results - Final analysis POST /api/text-generator - Dynamic text creation
{/* Adaptive Typing Test Section */}

Adaptive Typing Test Framework

The Adaptive Typing Test represents a breakthrough in personalized keyboard layout assessment. This sophisticated system employs algorithmic text generation, real-time weakness profiling, and Bayesian inference to create the most accurate individual typing assessment available.

1

5-Stage Sequential Testing

Each test runs for exactly 60 seconds with progressively adapted content. After each stage, the system analyzes your performance across 17 metrics, identifying weak fingers, problematic key combinations, and error patterns.

Test Progression:

  • • Stage 1: Baseline assessment with common English words
  • • Stage 2-4: Adaptive content targeting identified weaknesses
  • • Stage 5: Comprehensive evaluation combining all weak areas
2

Real-time Weakness Detection

Advanced algorithms track finger-specific performance, identifying weak fingers based on accuracy, speed, and consistency. The system also detects problematic words that cause frequent errors or significant slowdowns.

Analysis Factors:

  • • Finger-specific accuracy rates and error patterns
  • • Word-level difficulty assessment and retry frequency
  • • Typing rhythm consistency and flow disruptions
3

Adaptive Text Generation Engine

The system employs sophisticated algorithmic text generation using curated finger-specific word banks, bigram-targeted collections, and flow-optimized sequences. Each test stage strategically selects content to target your identified weaknesses.

Generation Strategy:

  • • Stage 2: Words targeting weakest fingers from finger-specific banks
  • • Stage 3: Bigram-focused content from problematic letter combinations
  • • Stage 4: Flow-testing sequences for hand alternation and rhythm
  • • Stage 5: Comprehensive synthesis combining all weakness areas
4

Intelligent Layout Recommendations

After completing all five stages, the system generates personalized keyboard layout recommendations. Using your unique weakness profile, it suggests layouts that minimize strain on your weak fingers while optimizing overall typing efficiency.

Recommendation Engine:

  • • Layout scoring based on individual finger strengths
  • • Weak finger workload minimization
  • • Alternative layout suggestions with detailed justification

Scientific Foundation

The adaptive testing methodology is inspired by computerized adaptive testing (CAT) used in educational assessment and psychological research. By dynamically adjusting test difficulty based on real-time performance, we achieve more accurate individual assessments in less time than traditional fixed-content tests.

)} {/* Process Framework Tab */} {activeTab === 'process' && (
{/* Framework Overview */}

Multi-Stage Analysis Framework

Comprehensive computational approach to layout evaluation

Our analysis framework combines multiple computational approaches to provide the most comprehensive keyboard layout evaluation available. Each stage builds upon the previous, creating a complete picture of typing performance, ergonomics, and efficiency.

Text Analysis

1M+ words processed for frequency analysis

Biomechanics

Finger strength and movement modeling

Pattern Detection

Advanced algorithms for movement analysis

Statistics

Weighted aggregation and normalization

{/* Stage 1: Text Processing */}
1

Comprehensive Text Analysis

Foundation layer for all subsequent calculations

Data Sources & Processing

Primary Corpus

  • • English literature from Project Gutenberg
  • • Technical writing and documentation
  • • News articles and contemporary prose
  • • Over 1 million words processed

Frequency Analysis

  • • Character frequency mapping
  • • Bigram occurrence statistics
  • • Trigram flow pattern analysis
  • • Statistical validation across sources

Processing Pipeline

1

Text Normalization

Convert to lowercase, filter non-alphabetic characters

2

Sequence Extraction

Extract all bigrams and trigrams with frequency counting

3

Statistical Weighting

Apply frequency weights for realistic usage modeling

4

Corpus Validation

Cross-validate frequencies across multiple text types

{/* Stage 2: Biomechanical Modeling */}
2

Biomechanical Modeling

Precise finger assignment and movement analysis

Finger Assignment

Standard Touch Typing

LP: Q, A, Z
RP: P, ;, /
LR: W, S, X
RR: O, L, .
LM: E, D, C
RM: I, K, ,
LI: R, F, V, T, G, B
RI: U, J, M, Y, H, N

Each key precisely mapped to anatomically correct finger usage

Effort Calculation

Difficulty Multipliers

Home Row: 1.0x (baseline)
Top Row: 1.3x (reach up)
Bottom Row: 1.2x (reach down)
Center Cols: 1.4x (awkward reach)

Research-based multipliers accounting for finger strength and reach

Distance Tracking

Euclidean Distance

d = √((x₂-x₁)² + (y₂-y₁)²)

Precise coordinate-based movement tracking

Real finger travel distances between all key combinations

{/* Stage 3: Advanced Pattern Detection */}
3

Advanced Pattern Detection

Sophisticated algorithms for movement and flow analysis

Bigram Analysis Engine

Same Finger Detection

Identifies letter pairs requiring consecutive use of the same finger - the most detrimental pattern for typing speed and accuracy.

if (finger1 === finger2) {'=>'} sameFingerBigram++

Skip Pattern Recognition

Detects awkward movements that skip keys on the same row.

if (sameRow && |col1 - col2| === 2) {'=>'} skipBigram++

Lateral Stretch Analysis

Identifies uncomfortable stretching motions within the same hand.

if (sameHand && differentFingers && |col1 - col2| {'>'} 2) {'=>'} stretch++

Trigram Flow Analysis

Roll Detection

Natural rolling motions within the same hand that feel smooth and fast.

if (sameHand && sameRow && rollingDirection) {'=>'} roll++

Alternation Patterns

Three-letter sequences that alternate between hands (L-R-L or R-L-R).

if (hand1 ≠ hand2 && hand2 ≠ hand3) {'=>'} alternation++

Redirect Analysis

Awkward direction changes that disrupt typing flow.

if ((dir1 {'>'} 0 && dir2 {'<'} 0) || (dir1 {'<'} 0 && dir2 {'>'} 0)) {'=>'} redirect++
{/* Stage 4: Statistical Aggregation */}
4

Statistical Aggregation & Normalization

Frequency-weighted calculations and comparative scoring

Weighted Aggregation

Core Formula

metric = Σ(frequency[pattern] × value[pattern]) / Σ(frequency[pattern])

Why Frequency Weighting?

  • • Reflects real-world typing patterns
  • • Prioritizes common letter combinations
  • • Provides statistically valid results
  • • Enables meaningful layout comparisons

Normalization & Scoring

Percentage Conversion

Most metrics converted to percentages for intuitive interpretation and comparison across layouts.

Ratio Calculations

Distance and effort metrics normalized as ratios to enable direct layout comparisons.

Statistical Validation

Results cross-validated across multiple text corpora for consistency and reliability.

{/* Performance & Optimization */}

Performance & Optimization

Caching Strategy

  • • Pre-computed frequency tables
  • • Cached bigram/trigram patterns
  • • Memoized distance calculations
  • • Result storage for reuse

Real-time Processing

  • • Debounced live analysis
  • • Progressive calculation updates
  • • Optimized algorithm complexity
  • • Sub-100ms response times

Scalability

  • • Memory-efficient data structures
  • • Parallel processing capabilities
  • • Database-free operation mode
  • • Horizontal scaling support
)} {/* Metrics Deep Dive Tab */} {activeTab === 'metrics' && (
{/* Hero Section */}

Complete Metric Definitions

Comprehensive analysis of all 17 metrics used in keyboard layout evaluation. Each metric includes precise formulas, implementation details, and research-backed target ranges for optimal typing performance.

17
Core Metrics
4
Categories
1M+
Text Analysis
30
Key Positions
{/* Detailed Metrics Grid Layout */}
{/* Ergonomic & Physical Metrics */}

Ergonomic & Physical Metrics

Biomechanical strain, finger load, and movement efficiency

{/* Effort */}

Total Effort

Physical typing strain

< 1.15 excellent

Measures the cumulative physical strain required to type text, accounting for key difficulty, finger strength differences, and character frequency in natural language.

Mathematical Formula

effort = Σ(rowDifficulty × colDifficulty × frequency) / totalChars
Where row difficulty: Top(1.3x), Home(1.0x), Bottom(1.2x) • Column difficulty: Center(1.4x), Others(1.0x)

Impact on Typing

Lower effort scores directly correlate with reduced finger fatigue, longer typing sessions without strain, and improved ergonomic health over time.

{/* Distance */}

Finger Travel

Movement distance

< 1.6 excellent

Calculates the average Euclidean distance fingers travel between consecutive keystrokes, using precise physical key coordinates for accurate movement analysis.

Mathematical Formula

distance = Σ√((x₂-x₁)² + (y₂-y₁)²) / totalTransitions
Euclidean distance calculation using precise key coordinates on standard QWERTY layout

Impact on Typing

Shorter travel distances enable faster typing speeds, reduce physical fatigue, and minimize the risk of repetitive strain injuries.

{/* Pinky Distance */}

Pinky Load

Weakest finger usage

< 15% ideal

Measures the proportion of total finger travel performed by the pinkies (weakest fingers), critical for preventing RSI and maintaining long-term typing comfort.

Mathematical Formula

pinky_ratio = pinkyTravelDistance / totalTravelDistance
Includes both left and right pinky movements across all typing sequences

Ergonomic Importance

Pinkies are the weakest and least dexterous fingers. Excessive pinky usage is a primary cause of typing-related repetitive strain injuries.

{/* Pinky Off-Home */}

Pinky Positioning

Home row accuracy

< 30% target

Percentage of pinky keystrokes that occur away from the home row position, indicating how often pinkies must leave their optimal resting position.

Mathematical Formula

off_home_pct = (offHomeKeystrokes / totalPinkyKeystrokes) × 100
Home position: A and ; keys for left and right pinkies respectively

Accuracy Benefits

Keeping pinkies on home row improves typing accuracy and reduces the cognitive load of finger positioning, leading to more consistent performance.

{/* Movement & Precision Metrics */}

Movement & Precision Metrics

Bigram analysis, finger coordination, and accuracy patterns

{/* Same Finger Bigrams */}

Same Finger Bigrams

Consecutive same finger use

< 2% ideal

Letter pairs requiring the same finger consecutively - the most detrimental pattern for typing speed, as one finger cannot press two keys simultaneously.

Mathematical Formula

sfb_pct = (sameFingerBigrams / totalBigrams) × 100
Where finger1 === finger2 for consecutive character pair

Impact on Typing

Same finger usage severely slows typing speed and dramatically increases error rates. Modern optimized layouts achieve <1% SFB rates.

{/* Skip Bigrams */}

Skip Bigrams

Key-skipping movements

< 3% target

Movements that skip one key on the same row, creating awkward finger positioning and potential for misfires during rapid typing.

Mathematical Formula

skip_pct = (skipBigrams / totalBigrams) × 100
Where pos1[0] === pos2[0] && |pos1[1] - pos2[1]| === 2

Impact on Typing

Skip movements feel unnatural and disrupt typing rhythm, leading to increased likelihood of typos and reduced flow.

{/* Lateral Stretch */}

Lateral Stretches

Uncomfortable hand stretching

< 5% target

Uncomfortable stretching motions within the same hand that can cause strain and increase the risk of repetitive stress injuries over time.

Mathematical Formula

lateral_pct = (lateralStretch / totalBigrams) × 100
Where sameHand && differentFingers && |pos1[1] - pos2[1]| > 2

Ergonomic Impact

Excessive lateral stretching can contribute to RSI development and should be minimized for long-term typing health.

{/* Pinky Scissors */}

Pinky Scissors

Awkward finger crossing

< 0.5% ideal

Awkward pinky-ring finger crossing movements that feel unnatural and significantly disrupt typing flow and accuracy.

Mathematical Formula

scissors_pct = (pinkyScissors / totalBigrams) × 100
Where isPinkyRing && sameHand && crossing motion detected

Impact on Typing

Scissoring motions are extremely error-prone and feel awkward, causing significant slowdowns in typing rhythm.

{/* Row Jumps */}

Row Jumps

Vertical movement disruption

< 8% target

Bigrams requiring large vertical movements (2+ rows) that disrupt natural finger positioning and slow down typing rhythm.

Mathematical Formula

two_row_pct = (twoRowJumps / totalBigrams) × 100
Where |pos1[0] - pos2[0]| >= 2 (any finger combination)

Impact on Typing

Large vertical movements slow typing and increase error rates by disrupting natural hand positioning and finger muscle memory.

{/* Skip Bigrams 2u */}

Skip Bigrams 2u

Extended key skips

< 1% critical

Movements that skip two keys on the same row, creating extreme hand contortion and significant typing disruption - worse than regular skips.

Mathematical Formula

skip2_pct = (skipBigrams2 / totalBigrams) × 100
Where pos1[0] === pos2[0] && |pos1[1] - pos2[1]| === 3

Critical Impact

Extended skips cause severe hand contortion and should be avoided entirely in any well-designed keyboard layout.

{/* Flow & Rhythm Metrics */}

Flow & Rhythm Metrics

Natural typing patterns, hand alternation, and movement fluidity

{/* Inward Rolls */}

Inward Rolls

Natural flow sequences

> 15% ideal

Natural finger movements rolling toward the keyboard center that create smooth, fast typing sequences and improve overall flow.

Mathematical Formula

roll_in_pct = (rollInBigrams / totalBigrams) × 100
Where sameHand && sameRow && rolling toward center

Impact on Typing

Natural rolling motions increase typing speed and feel effortless. Higher roll rates correlate with faster, more comfortable typing.

{/* Outward Rolls */}

Outward Rolls

Away from center flow

5-15% balanced

Finger movements rolling away from the keyboard center. While less optimal than inward rolls, moderate levels can still contribute to typing flow.

Mathematical Formula

roll_out_pct = (rollOutBigrams / totalBigrams) × 100
Where sameHand && sameRow && rolling outward from center

Impact on Typing

While not as smooth as inward rolls, balanced outward rolls can still contribute positively when not excessive.

{/* Trigram Alternation */}

Hand Alternation

L-R-L or R-L-R patterns

> 60% excellent

Three-letter sequences that alternate between hands, allowing one hand to rest while the other types, enabling extremely fast speeds.

Mathematical Formula

tri_alt_pct = (altTrigrams / totalTrigrams) × 100
Where hand1 ≠ hand2 && hand2 ≠ hand3 (full alternation)

Speed Benefits

Hand alternation enables the fastest possible typing speeds by allowing continuous motion while each hand rests alternately.

{/* Trigram Redirects */}

Direction Changes

Flow disruptions

< 20% target

Three-letter patterns requiring awkward direction changes that disrupt natural typing flow and rhythm patterns.

Mathematical Formula

redirect_pct = (redirectTrigrams / totalTrigrams) × 100
Where (dir1 > 0 && dir2 < 0) || (dir1 < 0 && dir2 > 0)

Flow Impact

Direction changes interrupt natural movement patterns and create hesitation in typing rhythm.

{/* Position and Usage Metrics */}

Key Position & Usage

Column 5,6 Usage %

Usage percentage of the center columns (T/Y and G/H positions in QWERTY).

Formula:

col56_pct = (col56Keystrokes / totalKeystrokes) × 100 where pos[1] === 4 || pos[1] === 5 (0-indexed)

Why It Matters:

Center columns are harder to reach and less accurate. Minimizing usage improves overall layout accessibility.

Target: < 8% (context dependent)

Two Row SFB %

Same finger bigrams that also require 2+ row jumps (worst case scenario).

Formula:

two_row_sfb_pct = (twoRowSfb / totalSfb) × 100 where finger1 === finger2 && |pos1[0] - pos2[0]| >= 2

Why It Matters:

Combines worst aspects of same finger bigrams and row jumping. Extremely detrimental to speed and comfort.

Target: < 10% of total SFBs
)} {/* Technical Reference Tab */} {activeTab === 'technical' && (
{/* Technical Overview */}

Complete Technical Reference

Comprehensive system architecture and implementation details

This section provides complete technical documentation for developers, researchers, and advanced users. Covering everything from algorithmic implementation details to API specifications and deployment architecture.

Source Code

Open source implementation details

Architecture

Full-stack system design patterns

APIs

RESTful endpoints and data schemas

Configuration

Setup and deployment guidelines

{/* System Architecture */}

System Architecture Overview

Frontend Architecture

React + TypeScript Stack

  • • React 18: Component-based UI with concurrent features
  • • TypeScript: Type-safe development with strict mode
  • • Tailwind CSS: Utility-first styling with dark mode
  • • Vite: Fast development server and build tool

State Management

  • • React Context: Global state for layout data
  • • Local State: Component-level UI state
  • • Custom Hooks: Reusable state logic
  • • Error Boundaries: Graceful error handling

Backend Architecture

Node.js + Express

  • • Express.js: RESTful API framework
  • • SQLite: Lightweight database for caching
  • • CORS: Cross-origin resource sharing
  • • Compression: Gzip response compression

Analysis Engines

  • • Core Analyzer: Original cyanophage-based engine
  • • Enhanced Analyzer: Performance-optimized version
  • • AI Descriptor: Layout description generator
  • • Recommendation Engine: Personalized suggestions
{/* Core Algorithms */}

Core Algorithm Implementation

Layout Analysis Engine

Bigram Processing

function analyzeBigrams(text, layout) {'{'} const bigrams = extractBigrams(text); let metrics = initializeMetrics(); for (const [bigram, freq] of bigrams) {'{'} const analysis = analyzeBigram(bigram, layout); updateMetrics(metrics, analysis, freq); {'}'} return normalizeMetrics(metrics); {'}'}

Distance Calculation

function calculateDistance(pos1, pos2) {'{'} const dx = pos2[0] - pos1[0]; const dy = pos2[1] - pos1[1]; return Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy); {'}'} function getEffort(row, col) {'{'} return ROW_DIFFICULTY[row] * COL_DIFFICULTY[col]; {'}'}

Pattern Recognition

Same Finger Detection

if (finger1 === finger2) {'{'} sameFingerCount++; {'}'}

Roll Detection

const isRoll = sameHand && sameRow && isRollingMotion; return isRoll;

Stretch Analysis

const stretch = sameHand && Math.abs(col1 - col2) {'>'} 2; return stretch;
{/* API Documentation */}

REST API Documentation

Core Analysis Endpoints

POST /api/analyze

Analyze a keyboard layout and return comprehensive metrics

Request Body:
{'{'} "layout": ["qwerty..."], "text": "sample text", "options": {'{...}'} {'}'}
Response:
{'{'} "effort": 1.23, "distance": 1.45, "sfb_pct": 2.1, "roll_in_pct": 18.5 {'}'}
GET /api/layouts

Retrieve all saved keyboard layouts with their cached analysis results

[{'{'} "id": 1, "name": "QWERTY", "layout": [...], "analysis": {'{...}'} {'}'} ]
POST /api/layouts/describe

Generate AI-powered description and analysis of a keyboard layout

{'{'} "description": "This is a...", "archetype": "balanced", "strengths": [...], "weaknesses": [...] {'}'}

Adaptive Testing Endpoints

POST /api/adaptive-test/start

Initialize new adaptive typing test session

{'{'} "sessionId": "abc123", "stage": 1, "text": "baseline..." {'}'}
POST /api/adaptive-test/submit

Submit test results and get next stage

{'{'} "nextStage": 2, "text": "adaptive...", "weaknesses": [...] {'}'}
{/* Database Schema */}

Database Schema & Data Models

Core Tables

layouts

CREATE TABLE layouts ( id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, keys TEXT NOT NULL, layout_data TEXT, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );

layout_stats

CREATE TABLE layout_stats ( layout_id INTEGER, effort REAL, distance REAL, sfb_pct REAL, roll_in_pct REAL, FOREIGN KEY(layout_id) REFERENCES layouts(id) );

Adaptive Testing

typing_sessions

CREATE TABLE typing_sessions ( id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, user_id TEXT, layout_name TEXT, stage INTEGER, results TEXT, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );

text_corpora

CREATE TABLE text_corpora ( id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, content TEXT NOT NULL, bigrams TEXT, trigrams TEXT, frequencies TEXT );
{/* Performance Optimization */}

Performance Optimization Techniques

Caching Strategies

Frequency Tables

Pre-computed bigram/trigram frequencies stored in SQLite

Layout Results

Analysis results cached with layout hash keys

Memory Cache

In-memory LRU cache for frequent computations

Algorithm Optimization

Early Termination

Skip redundant calculations for known patterns

Vectorized Ops

Batch process multiple bigrams simultaneously

Lazy Loading

Load text corpora only when needed

Scalability Features

Worker Threads

CPU-intensive analysis in separate threads

Request Queuing

Rate limiting and priority queue management

Horizontal Scale

Stateless design enables load balancing

{/* Deployment Guide */}

Deployment & Configuration

Development Setup

Prerequisites

  • • Node.js 18+ and npm/yarn
  • • Git for version control
  • • SQLite3 (included with Node.js)
  • • Modern web browser for testing

Installation Commands

# Clone repository git clone https://github.com/user/layoutgod.git cd layoutgod # Install dependencies npm install # Start development servers npm run dev

Production Deployment

Environment Variables

NODE_ENV=production PORT=3000 DATABASE_URL=./data/layouts.db CORS_ORIGIN=https://yourdomain.com API_RATE_LIMIT=100

Build Process

# Frontend build cd client && npm run build # Backend start cd server && npm start # Or use PM2 pm2 start ecosystem.config.js

Research Foundation

Corpus Linguistics

My analysis is grounded in computational linguistics, using publicly available text corpora to determine character and sequence frequencies:

  • • 1M+ words from English literature and articles
  • • 500K+ words from programming code repositories
  • • Statistical validation across multiple text types
  • • Dynamic weighting based on user context

Biomechanical Modeling

I incorporate established research on hand anatomy and ergonomics:

  • • Finger strength ratios based on biomechanical studies
  • • Range of motion limitations for each finger
  • • Fatigue models for repetitive strain prediction
  • • Hand posture considerations for natural positioning

Mathematical Framework

Frequency-Weighted Analysis

Every metric is calculated using statistical weighting based on real text frequencies:

metric_score = Σ(frequency[pattern] × difficulty[pattern]) / Σ(frequency[pattern])

Bigram Analysis

676 possible letter pairs analyzed for movement patterns

Trigram Analysis

17,576 three-letter patterns for flow and alternation

Position Analysis

30-key layout with finger assignments and reach distances

)} {/* Footer */}

Open Source & Research Based

My analysis methodology is based on the open-source cyanophage analyzer and academic research in ergonomics and biomechanics. All calculations are transparent and can be verified through the source code.

MIT Licensed Research Validated Community Driven
